title: K-200 Structural Specification v2.0
text:
# K-200 Structural Specification

Document number: MD-DOC-409-B. Revision: B. Type: specification. Author: Tobias Wren. Created: 2023-04-10. Last modified: 2023-10-15. Status: Released.

## Change History
- Rev A: Initial structural spec, CFRP wing skins (2023-04-01)
- Rev B: Wing skin material change to hybrid carbon-aramid composite per ECN-307 / ECR-207 (2023-10-15)

## §3 Wing Skin (P-1008, P-1009)

Material: Hybrid carbon-aramid composite — CFRP weave (0°/90°) + Kevlar® ±45° interlayer + CFRP weave (0°/90°). Total thickness: 1.4mm nominal.

Rationale for material change: Straight CFRP (0°/90°) wing skins (P-1008/P-1009 Rev B) delaminated at the wing root bond line after 12 hours of simulated motor vibration loading during ground vibration test (KES-045). Material coupon tests in KES-042 confirmed hybrid carbon-aramid has no delamination at 100 hours (vs CFRP failure at 12 hours). The aramid interlayer provides crack arrest. Mass penalty: +25g per skin. Design review: WIKI-017. Change authority: ECN-307.

The stiffness of the hybrid composite is approximately 8% lower than straight CFRP. Loads analysis re-run in DOC-407 confirmed this reduction is acceptable against flutter margins, with flutter onset at 52m/s (requirement: >45m/s at maximum dive speed).

## §4 Motor Mounts (P-1012–P-1015)

Aluminium 6061-T6. Stiffness requirement: natural frequency of motor mount assembly >200Hz (to avoid coupling with motor vibration spectrum). Verified in DRW-507.
